LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMUDEFENDANT ACA INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. AS TO THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE, IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE LAW. AS TO THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING CAUSES OF ACTION, AS TO WHICH THE OPPOSITION IS SILENT, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. BAD FAITH, RATHER THAN NEGLIGENCE, IS THE ONLY BASIS FOR AN INSURED'S CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE INSURER FOR DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THE INSURER'S HANDLING OF A CLAIM. (BROWN V. GUARANTEE INS. CO. (1957) 155 CAL.APP.2D 679, 688-89.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ACA INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied as moot, in light of the ruling on demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ACA INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained. As to the Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend if Plaintiff can allege, in good faith, facts establishing that Defendant engaged in outrageous conduct within the meaning of the law. As to the Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress and Negligent Breach of the Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing causes of action, as to which the Opposition is silent, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Bad faith, rather than negligence, is the only basis for an insured's cause of action against the insurer for damages arising out of the insurer's handling of a claim. (Brown v. Guarantee Ins. Co. (1957) 155 Cal.App.2d 679, 688-89.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
